50.reason: Increment count of unique words only when adding a new one

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  This line increments i unconditionally after the loop, even when the word already exists. This causes empty entries in the array and incorrect counting. The increment should only happen when a new word is added.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  This line increments i unconditionally, even when the word already exists in the array, causing empty entries and incorrect indexing.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Unconditionally increments i even when the word already existed, causing empty entries in the array
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  错误：无论是否添加新单词都递增 i，导致数组中出现未初始化的元素，并第一次遇到单词时因 i=0 循环不执行而丢失单词
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  Increment count of unique words only when adding a new one
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Increment the word count only when a new word is added.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [i is incremented regardless of whether the word was found or not. If the word already exists, i still increases, creating empty/zero-count entries that get printed later.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [i is incremented even when the word already exists (due to break), leading to skipped slots and uninitialized entries. Also, when i==0, the for loop doesn't run, so no word is added but i becomes 1, leaving a[0] uninitialized.]
